title: Chicken and mushroom pie
subtitle: A classic British pie with a short pastry lid and a creamy filling
excerpt: Chicken and mushroom pie has a short pastry lid over a filling of poached chicken and mushrooms in a cream sauce. The pastry is baked until golden and the filling, made separately on the hob, is set firm enough to cut cleanly once the pie has rested. It is a make-ahead pie: better on the second day.
sourceNotes: Chicken and mushroom pie is a standard of British domestic cookery, documented in household cooking guides throughout the twentieth century. The formula here follows the classic method: poached chicken, sautéed mushrooms, a roux-based cream sauce, and a short pastry lid. Isabella Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg, documents similar chicken pie preparations.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze assembled and unbaked. Bake from frozen at 180°C fan, adding 20 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make the filling up to 2 days ahead and refrigerate. Top with pastry and bake on the day.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the pastry lid needs high heat to colour

content.text: Chicken and mushroom pie is a deep pie dish filled with chicken and mushrooms in a cream sauce, topped with short pastry and baked until golden. The filling is cooked on the hob before it goes into the dish, so the bake is really about setting the filling and cooking the pastry. A pie funnel in the centre supports the lid and lets steam escape.

Rub the cold butter into the flour and salt until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs. Add the cold water a tablespoon at a time and bring together into a dough. Do not overwork. Wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Cut the chicken thighs into 3 cm pieces. Melt the butter in a wide saucepan over medium-high heat. Brown the chicken in batches, set aside. Soften the onion in the same pan for 5 minutes. Add the mushrooms and cook until their moisture has evaporated. Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the stock gradually, stirring, and bring to a simmer. Add the cream and thyme. Return the chicken. Simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce has thickened and coats the back of a spoon. Season and cool slightly.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Pour the filling into a deep pie dish. Roll the pastry to about 4 mm thick and cut a lid slightly larger than the dish. Press the pastry lid onto the dish, crimp the edges with a fork or finger, and cut a central steam vent. Brush with beaten egg. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the pastry is deep gold and the filling is bubbling at the vent. Rest for 15 minutes before serving.
